revision.text: {{{3}}} |title="{{{1}}} transliteration">{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates will still be usable, but they will just transclude this one. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. **{{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ISO). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. **{{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if {{{3|}}} |{{{3}}}|{{{2}}} }} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. {{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates will still be usable, but they will just transclude this one. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. **{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ISO). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. **[[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} ** [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates will still be usable, but they will just transclude this one. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ISO). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates will still be usable, but they will just transclude this one (e.g. {{IAST|saṃskṛtam}} will be a shortcut for {{transl|sa|IAST|saṃskṛtam}}). There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ISO). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. {{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates are still usable, but they just transclude this one (e.g. {{IAST|saṃskṛtam}} is a shortcut for {{transl|sa|IAST|saṃskṛtam}}). At present, this template is explicitly aware of individual css classes: *"IAST" *"Arabic Unicode" *"SpanHe" pending a smarter solution in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]]. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates are still usable, but they just transclude this one (e.g. {{IAST|saṃskṛtam}} is a shortcut for {{transl|sa|IAST|saṃskṛtam}}). At present, this template is explicitly aware of individual css classes: *"IAST" *"Arabic Unicode" *"Semitic Unicode" pending a smarter solution in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]]. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates are still usable, but they just transclude this one (e.g. {{IAST|saṃskṛtam}} is a shortcut for {{transl|sa|IAST|saṃskṛtam}}). At present, this template is explicitly aware of individual css classes: *"latinx" (default) *"IAST" (from {{tl|IAST}}) *"Arabic Unicode" (from {{tl|ArabDIN}}) *"Semitic Unicode" (fropm {{tl|semxlit}}) pending a smarter solution in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]]. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates are still usable, but they just transclude this one (e.g. {{IAST|saṃskṛtam}} is a shortcut for {{transl|sa|IAST|saṃskṛtam}}). At present, this template is explicitly aware of individual css classes: *"latinx" (default) *"IAST" (from {{tl|IAST}}) *"Arabic Unicode" (from {{tl|ArabDIN}}) *"Semitic Unicode" (fropm {{tl|semxlit}}) pending a smarter solution in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]]. There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

{{ #if: {{{3|}}}|{{{3}}}|{{{2}}}}} {{protected template}} ==Usage== This template is intended to ultimately replace all "transliteration" templates (like {{tl|IAST}}, {{tl|ISOtranslit}}, {{tl|ArabDIN}}. These templates are still usable, but they just transclude this one (e.g. {{IAST|saṃskṛtam}} is a shortcut for {{transl|sa|IAST|saṃskṛtam}}). At present, this template is explicitly aware of individual css classes: *"latinx" (default) *"IAST" (from {{tl|IAST}}) *"Arabic Unicode" (from {{tl|ArabDIN}}) *"Semitic Unicode" (fropm {{tl|semxlit}}) pending a smarter solution in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]]. This template is kept separate from {{tl|lang}} to address formatting issues (via css classes) and identification of transliteration schemes used. Ultimately, if these concerns are smartly addressed in the css file and/or in {{tl|lang}}, using {{transl|xx|...}} should be equivalent to using {{lang|xx-Latn|...}}, viz. stating that the string is a Romanization by adding "-Latn" to the language code. ---- There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. examples: *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]]

Ultimately, if these concerns are smartly addressed in the css file and/or in {{tl|lang}}, using {{transl|xx|...}} should be equivalent to using {{lang|xx-Latn|...}}, viz. stating that the string is a Romanization by adding "-Latn" to the language code. ---- There are two ways of using this template: with or without specifying the transliteration scheme used: *two parameters: {{transl|ar|al-Khwarizmi}} means that "al-Khwarizmi" is a transliteration from the Arabic in a loose or unspecified scheme. *three parameters: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} the second parameter specifies the scheme used (DIN vs. ALA). {{transl|ar|ISO|...}} is equivalent to {{ISOtranslit|...|ar}}, and {{transl|ar|DIN|...}} to {{ArabDIN|...}}. ===Examples=== *{{transl|ar|[[al-Khwarizmi]]}} / [[DIN 31635]]: {{transl|ar|DIN|al-Ḫuwārizmī}} / [[ISO 233]]: {{transl|ar|ISO|al-H̱uwārizmī}} / [[ALA-LC Romanization|ALA]]: {{transl|ar|ALA|al-Khwārizmī}} * [[Royal Thai General System of Transcription|RTGST]]: {{transl|th|phasa thai}} / [[ISO 11940]]: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} '''ISO''': *{{lang||[[Russian language|русский]]}} {{transl|ru|ISO|russkij}}: {{transl|ru|ISO|russkij}} *{{lang|ab|[[Abkhaz language|Аҧсуа]]}} {{transl|ab|ISO|aṗsua}}: {{transl|ab|ISO|aṗsua}} *{{lang|ar|[[Arabic language| عربي ]]}} {{transl|ar|ISO|ʿarabī}}: {{transl|ar|ISO|ʿarabī}} *{{lang|ta|[[Tamil language|தமிழ் ]]}} {{transl|ta|ISO|tamiḻ}}: {{transl|ta|ISO|tamiḻ}} *{{lang|bn|[[Bengali language|বাংলা ]]}} {{transl|bn|ISO|baṅla}}: {{transl|bn|ISO|baṅla}} *{{lang|el|[[Greek language|ελληνικά ]]}} {{transl|el|ISO|ellēniká}}: {{transl|el|ISO|ellēniká}} *[[Georgian language|{{lang|ka|ქართული}}]] {{transl|ka|ISO|k'art'uli}}: {{transl|ka|ISO|k'art'uli}} *{{lang|zh|[[Pinyin|漢語拼音]]}} {{transl|zh|ISO|hànyǔ pīnyīn}}: {{transl|zh|ISO|hànyǔ pīnyīn}}; compare: {{lang|pny|hànyǔ pīnyīn}}: {{lang|pny|hànyǔ pīnyīn}} *{{lang|th|[[Thai language|ภาษาไทย]]}} {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}}: {{transl|th|ISO|p̣hās̄ʹāthịy}} ==See also== *{{tl|lang}} [[Category:Multilingual support templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Esoteric templates]]

revision.comment: Undid revision 820209795 by [[Special:Contributions/NeilN|NeilN]] ([[User talk:NeilN|talk]]) TTM says fixed
revision.text:
<includeonly>{{#invoke:lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
</noinclude>

<includeonly>{{#invoke:Lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
</noinclude>

<includeonly>{{#invoke:Lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
<templatedata>
{
	"params": {
		"transl": {
			"label": "Transliteration",
			"description": "The transliterated text",
			"example": "Kyivska oblast",
			"type": "string",
			"required": true
		},
		"code": {
			"label": "Language code",
			"description": "ISO 639 language code",
			"example": "uk",
			"type": "string",
			"suggested": true
		},
		"translit-script": {
			"label": "Script code",
			"description": "ISO15924 script code",
			"example": "Cyrl",
			"type": "string"
		},
		"transl-std": {
			"label": "Standard",
			"description": "The transliteration standard or scheme used",
			"example": "ungegn",
			"type": "string"
		}
	},
	"description": "Marks text as a transliteration from one writing system to another. Either language code or script code must be set.",
	"format": "inline"
}
</templatedata></noinclude>

revision.comment: Well that didn’t work at all
revision.text:
<includeonly>{{#invoke:Lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
<templatedata>
{
	"params": {},
	"description": "Marks text as a transliteration from one writing system to another. Either language code or script code must be set.",
	"format": "inline"
}
</templatedata></noinclude>

<includeonly>{{#invoke:Lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
<templatedata>
{
	"description": "Indicates that a span of text is transliterated from a particular language or writing system, and, optionally, according to a specific transliteration system.",
	"params": {
		"1": {
			"label": "Language or script code",
			"description": "ISO 639 language code or ISO 15924 script code",
			"required": true,
			"type": "line"
		},
		"2": {
			"label": "Text or transliteration scheme",
			"description": "Source text to be transliterated, or, optionally, transliteration scheme",
			"required": true,
			"type": "string"
		},
		"3": {
			"label": "Text",
			"description": "Source text to be transliterated (required if a transliteration scheme is entered above)",
			"required": false,
			"type": "string"
		}
	}
}
</templatedata></noinclude>

<includeonly>{{#invoke:Lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
<templatedata>
{
	"description": "Indicates that a span of text is transliterated from a particular language or writing system, and, optionally, according to a specific transliteration system.",
	"params": {
		"1": {
			"label": "Language or script code",
			"description": "ISO 639 language code or ISO 15924 script code",
			"required": true,
			"type": "line"
		},
		"2": {
			"label": "Text or transliteration scheme",
			"description": "Source text to be transliterated, or, optionally, transliteration scheme",
			"required": true,
			"type": "string"
		},
		"3": {
			"label": "Text",
			"description": "Source text to be transliterated (required if a transliteration scheme is entered above)",
			"required": false,
			"suggested": true,
			"type": "string"
		}
	}
}
</templatedata></noinclude>

<includeonly>{{#invoke:Lang|transl}}</includeonly><noinclude>
{{documentation}}
<templatedata>
{
	"description": "Marks a text span transliterated from a particular language or writing system, and, optionally, according to a specific transliteration system.",
	"params": {
		"1": {
			"label": "Language or script code",
			"description": "ISO 639 language code (e.g., uk) or ISO 15924 script code (e.g., Cyrl)",
			"required": true,
			"type": "line"
		},
		"2": {
			"label": "Text or transliteration scheme",
			"description": "Source text to be transliterated, or, optionally, transliteration scheme (e.g., bgn/pcgn)",
			"required": true,
			"type": "string"
		},
		"3": {
			"label": "Text",
			"description": "Source text to be transliterated (required if a transliteration scheme is entered above)",
			"required": false,
			"suggested": true,
			"type": "string"
		}
	}
}
</templatedata></noinclude>
